title: Continental Operations — Infrastructure at Scale
prerequisites: Advanced Offensive Track - Mastery Tier Access
learning_objectives: Multi-region infrastructure architecture with engagement isolation
Terraform module development for IaC best practices
Health monitoring and infrastructure burn detection
African cloud provider cost analysis and selection
scenario: This Mastery mission requires professional-grade execution in Southern and East African multi-engagement red team operations. Challenge: Build automation-first infrastructure supporting 3 simultaneous engagements across regions with complete isolation. Relevance: Pan-African red team operations require cloud cost optimization and rapid infrastructure deployment

scenario_context.setting: Southern and East African multi-engagement red team operations
scenario_context.challenge: Build automation-first infrastructure supporting 3 simultaneous engagements across regions with complete isolation
scenario_context.relevance: Pan-African red team operations require cloud cost optimization and rapid infrastructure deployment

subtasks.title: Architecture Design
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Design multi-region infrastructure:
        - Team server placement: Where to host command and control?
        - Redirectors: How to proxy traffic through multiple layers?
        - Engagement isolation: How to separate 3 concurrent operations?
        - Network isolation: How to prevent cross-engagement contamination?

        Deliver: Architecture document with network diagrams.
subtasks.evidence_required: Architecture with network diagrams + isolation model

subtasks.title: Terraform Modules
subtasks.description: Modules: team server, HTTP redirector, DNS redirector, monitoring.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Develop Terraform modules:
        - Team server module: Deploy C2 infrastructure
        - HTTP redirector: Deploy HTTP/HTTPS redirector
        - DNS redirector: Deploy DNS redirector
        - Monitoring: Deploy monitoring and alerting

        Deliver: Terraform code, variables, output documentation.

subtasks.title: Domain & Certificate Automation
subtasks.description: Automated domain config and cert management.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Implement automation:
        - Domain registration and DNS configuration
        - SSL certificate provisioning (Let's Encrypt)
        - Automated renewal and rotation
        - Integration with infrastructure-as-code

        Deliver: Automation scripts, API integrations, deployment demo.

subtasks.title: Monitoring & Alerting
subtasks.description: Health monitoring and burn detection alerting.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Implement operational monitoring:
        - Health checks: Is infrastructure responding?
        - Burn detection: Are redirectors being burned/detected?
        - Alerting: Notify on infrastructure issues
        - Metrics: Track uptime and performance

        Deliver: Monitoring configuration, alert examples.

subtasks.title: Burn & Rebuild Procedure
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Test complete incident response:
        - Execute full burn procedure: shutdown, decommission, cleanup
        - Rebuild from scratch using Terraform
        - Measure time to recovery
        - Document lessons learned

        Deliver: Burn procedure documentation, timed rebuild evidence.

subtasks.title: Cost Analysis
subtasks.description: Compare African cloud providers. Optimise budget.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Analyze cloud provider options:
        - Compare: AWS, Azure, Google Cloud in African regions
        - Cost: Compute, bandwidth, data transfer
        - Coverage: Which regions support your operations?
        - Optimization: How to minimize costs?

        Deliver: Cost analysis comparing providers with recommendations.

resources.title: Mission Overview: Continental Operations — Infrastructure at Scale

reflection_prompt: Which African cloud providers offer the best combination of coverage, cost, and OPSEC for regional operations? How would you architect infrastructure to support parallel assessments in Nigeria, Kenya, and South Africa?

quiz.question: When designing multi-region infrastructure for concurrent red team engagements across Southern Africa, what is the primary purpose of engagement isolation?
options: To prevent compromise of one engagement from affecting other engagements or exposing the team's operational presence
To reduce the number of clients you can serve
Isolation is not necessary if you use the same infrastructure
quiz.explanation: Engagement isolation ensures that if one target detects and compromises your infrastructure, the other engagements remain unaffected. This protects your team's operational presence and prevents cross-contamination between sensitive client assessments.

quiz.question: In terms of burn-and-rebuild procedures for African cloud operations, what metric indicates your infrastructure automation is mature?
options: The infrastructure can be rebuilt in 1 minute
You can completely burn and rebuild infrastructure within 30 minutes with minimal manual steps
You don't need burn procedures if your infrastructure is expensive
quiz.explanation: Infrastructure-as-code maturity means you can redeploy everything automatically after compromise. The ability to execute complete burn-and-rebuild within 30 minutes provides operational resilience and allows teams to respond quickly to discovered infrastructure compromises.

portfolio_artifacts: Evidence of technical excellence and strategic thinking
mentor_notes:
This Mastery mission requires senior mentor review. Candidates must demonstrate:
- Professional-grade execution and documentation
- Deep understanding of African organizational and threat contexts
- Strategic thinking beyond tactical execution
- Readiness for commercial red team operations
- Leadership potential within OCH and broader community

Successful completion positions candidate for Mastery portfolio and professional practice.
